evidence: Consistent camelCase naming across all directories — no snake_case drift detected. naming_by_directory shows pure camelCase in every subdirectory
No default exports anywhere in src/ — all named exports consistently
All 20+ plugins follow create*Plugin factory pattern with consistent FrameworkPlugin return type

Plugins that wire into the extension system export bare hook functions (e.g., signalsBeforeSense, sourcesAfterPublishDraft) alongside the factory — consistent across sources, signals, predictions, tips, lifecycle
File naming is consistently kebab-case across all directories
Toolkit tools follow a uniform pattern: import types, import ok/err/demosError, import DemosSession, use withToolWrapper
One exact function duplication: normalizeFeedPosts in publish-pipeline.ts and feed-filter.ts
Guard files in toolkit/guards/ all follow consistent pattern: import state-helpers, define constants, export check* function
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: normalizeFeedPosts duplication between publish-pipeline.ts and feed-filter.ts. Plugin export pattern has a minor inconsistency where some plugins export hooks for two different consumption paths (extension dispatcher + FrameworkPlugin registry) without documenting why.

convention_outlier.dimension_character: The codebase demonstrates unusually strong convention discipline for a TypeScript project of this size. camelCase naming is universal across all directories, file naming is consistently kebab-case, all exports are named (no default exports), and the plugin system follows a uniform factory pattern. The toolkit tools share a consistent structural pattern with withToolWrapper, and guard modules share a consistent state-helpers-based pattern. The only meaningful convention outlier is the normalizeFeedPosts duplication between two files in different subsystems.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: A developer working here would find conventions highly predictable — patterns learned in one area reliably apply everywhere. The naming census shows pure camelCase across all 20+ directories. The plugin factory pattern, toolkit tool pattern, and guard pattern are all consistent. The normalizeFeedPosts duplication is a concrete outlier but is a single instance rather than a systemic problem. This places the codebase around 88, solidly in the 'strong with minor friction' range — the one duplication and the dual-export pattern in some plugins are isolated rough edges.

issues.summary: Exact duplicate function normalizeFeedPosts exists in publish-pipeline.ts and feed-filter.ts
evidence: Confirmed by desloppify show dupes: exact duplicate at publish-pipeline.ts:86 and feed-filter.ts:101
Both implementations are identical — same payload?.posts ?? payload?.results ?? ... chain
issues.suggestion: Delete the copy in publish-pipeline.ts and import from feed-filter.ts. This is a single-edit fix.

root_causes: normalizeFeedPosts was likely copied during the publish pipeline extraction from the original codebase rather than shared through an import

add.header: Universal camelCase naming across all directories
add.description: Every directory uses pure camelCase function/variable naming with zero snake_case drift. This is verified by the naming_by_directory census across 20+ directories. A developer can trust this convention universally.

add.header: All named exports, no default exports
add.description: No files in src/ use default exports. This eliminates the inconsistency between named and default import patterns that plagues many TypeScript codebases.

All 20+ plugins export a create*Plugin factory returning FrameworkPlugin. Plugins that also wire into the extension dispatcher additionally export bare hook functions. This dual-export is consistent across all hook-providing plugins.

All toolkit tools follow the same structure: type imports, ok/err/demosError imports, DemosSession import, withToolWrapper wrapping, validateInput schema check, then domain logic. This makes tools highly predictable to read and write.
All source files use kebab-case filenames (e.g., sdk-bridge.ts, write-rate-limit.ts, attestation-policy.ts). No PascalCase or camelCase file names exist.
